summary: Senior dApp Engineer and full-stack engineer with 12+ years building production TypeScript, React, Next.js, Node.js, and Web3 security products. Recent OpenZeppelin work spans Defender Secure Audit, OSS Relayer, SDK integrations, Solidity test-network contract work for audit-support workflows, EVM transaction handling, RPC retry and failover, gas alerting, and nonce race-condition fixes. Strong fit for teams that need resilient user-facing dApp surfaces, reliable Web3 UX, and cross-functional delivery with protocol, platform, SRE, audit, and customer-facing teams.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Co-led Defender 2.0 Secure Audit as one of two engineers, building a TypeScript and React module for smart contract audit delivery workflows, covering client collaboration, finding tracking, fix verification, and final PDF reports.
Built Defender and OSS Relayer SDK capabilities for Web3 developer integration workflows, helping technical teams integrate OpenZeppelin products and create custom automation tooling.

Owned EVM-side work for the Rust-based OpenZeppelin Relayer OSS project, improving RPC failover, signer integrations, and nonce reliability across chain-specific transaction handling, including a critical nonce race-condition fix.
Cut RPC and gas-spike alert noise by about 60%, making Slack and PagerDuty incidents easier to triage.

Wrote Solidity test-network contracts for audit workflows at OpenZeppelin, including integrations, source-code validation, Monitor checks for specific function calls or parameters, and AI-assisted checks for basic audit review.
Led Defender Playwright testing across critical user flows to protect release confidence on key paths.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Built a live-data React feature in MyPremise so account managers and clients could use shareable live data instead of static exports.
Updated Express.js API services during a REST-to-GraphQL migration with Apollo, improving API response times by 35% and lowering server usage.
Expanded Jest coverage and CI gates, reducing release cycles below 24 hours.

workExperience.title: Software Engineer
description: Built backend integrations for merchant ingestion pipelines, normalizing FTP, API, SOAP XML, and scraping data for pricing, stock, availability, bundles, and product attributes.
Led an integration and onboarding platform with Go, Node.js, and Angular, saving $75,000 annually and speeding up customer-service onboarding.
Drove unit testing for pricing, offers, and bundles logic with an 80% coverage target, reducing critical rollout defects by 33%.

workExperience.title: System Architect
description: Improved architecture and delivery flow for a legacy internal platform, cutting release cycles from months to about two weeks.
Implemented transaction-related API flows with Express.js, including Transbank payment-processing API integrations.
Led a staged migration from on-prem to Azure hybrid infrastructure across applications, databases, jobs, storage, and networking.

workExperience.title: Full Stack Developer
description: Delivered more than five web and mobile products for clients, working from discovery and requirements through planning and implementation.
Led TypeScript and React standards across the company, covering architecture, code review, and delivery practices.
Mentored five developers through pairing, code reviews, and workshops on architecture, testing, and code quality.

workExperience.title: Fullstack Developer
description: Focused on backend development across Python and Go services for search, geolocation, real-time operational features, and product ETL workflows.
Managed business metrics, performance monitoring, and deployment workflows to support continuous process improvements.

workExperience.title: Junior Software Developer
description: Started as a junior developer and took on frontend ownership, helping introduce Node.js and AngularJS into the agency stack.

education.degree: Bachelor of Engineering in Computer Science

company_profile.description: Golem Factory, based in Zug, Switzerland, builds Golem Network, an open-source decentralized computing platform where providers share computing power and requestors use distributed resources without relying on a centralized cloud provider. Arkiv, from the Golem ecosystem, is an Ethereum-aligned universal data layer for queryable, time-scoped, verifiable data.
company_profile.mission: Inferred from official Golem and Arkiv pages: make computing and data infrastructure more open, accessible, and user-controlled through decentralized networks.
company_profile.vision: Inferred from official sources: support a future where computation and data are open, shared, trustless by default, and useful to Web3 builders.
services: React and browser tooling for dApps
target_customers: Developers building browser and wallet-based dApps
Web2 teams exploring Web3 data infrastructure
company_profile.industry: Web3 infrastructure, decentralized computing, Ethereum data infrastructure
company_profile.business_model: Open-source decentralized network ecosystem using GLM, an ERC-20 token on Ethereum and Polygon. Arkiv uses GLM and ETH gas logic across its L2 and L3 architecture.
recent_signals: Golem 2024 goals include GPU power for AI/ML, protocol optimization for higher traffic, and more project development on Golem technology.
Arkiv evolved from Golem DB into an Ethereum-native data availability and management layer with query language support and expiry-based fees.
Arkiv positions DB-Chains as L3 database-like chains coordinated by an L2 Arkiv Chain built on OP Stack.
Official Arkiv docs show TypeScript/JavaScript SDK, React integration, public and wallet clients, and browser tutorials using MetaMask.

source_notes.claim: Golem Network is an open-source decentralized computing platform where users can share and access computing power without relying on centralized cloud providers.
Golem developer resources include JavaScript and Python docs plus creator, provider, Ray, and dApp documentation.
Arkiv is a universal data layer for Ethereum with queryable, time-scoped, verifiable data and DB-Chains coordinated by Arkiv Chain.
Arkiv documentation provides a JavaScript/TypeScript SDK, JSON-RPC API, network details, bridge contract, and wallet-related setup.
The mission and vision statements are inferred from official Golem and Arkiv positioning rather than quoted directly.
